creator: _jkrow
caption:
Three years ago, I started wearing @vivobarefoot shoes for hiking…but along the way they became my go to’s for pretty much everything | ad 

👣 Say ‘VIVO’ and I’ll send you the links to these new sales for spring!

A lot of people assume barefoot shoes are only for hardcore runners or hikers, but over the years I’ve learned that it’s way more about feeling connected to the places you’re in - and they all feel a lot different when your feet are allowed to move naturally 

🌿Slow mornings: Sensus II Grounding shoe
🥾Trail days: Primus Trail Knit FG
🔥 Around the fire: Tracker Ora Sandal

#vivobarefoot #barefootshoes

transcription.text: i started wearing barefoot shoes for hiking but honestly at this point they've become my go-to for pretty much everything i do outside. i always joke that they feel like sweatpants for your feet because your toes can actually move in them and once you get used to that feeling it's really hard to go back because you start to realize just how restrictive a lot of regular shoes actually are. i think people hear the term barefoot shoes and assume they're only for hardcore hikers or runners. i wear them just as much for slow mornings outside, cabins, and sitting around the fire after hikes. they make me feel more connected to where i am, like you can actually feel the ground instead of feeling disconnected all the time. i think that's why i love wearing them when i travel too because travel already makes you slow down and notice things more and these just add to that feeling. so whenever i pack for trips, vivo barefoot shoes are always the first thing i throw in my bag.
